claim: Heavy armour carries no wearer-side cost (no tempo/stamina/mobility/encumbrance term); it only ever modifies the opponent's exchange, so armour tier is a strictly dominant pick and its choice tree collapses.

impose_node gives counter/measure traditions a bind->riposte offensive reward while bind/burst/flow schools get a neutral or no imposition, tilting the tradition choice tree toward the 4 refuser traditions.

overcommit_exposure subtracts additive anti_overcommit + true_times from a floored multiplicative cost, driving net exposure to exactly 0 for a light hand-balanced weapon, removing the declared commit=give-up-recovery tradeoff.
Formal-Contest Recall (+2D binary, per-exchange) plus uncapped Corroborate/Prep/Findings channels dwarf the base attribute pool, so build choice collapses to bonus-stacking.
cite: social_contest_v30.md §4 Step 3 (v30:166,168) + §9.1; §2 Step 3 (v30:85 cap covers only genre+boost)

claim: Appraise's audience-boost read is a deterministic public lookup (faction table + engine Guilds rule), so the +1D-boost style dimension is pre-solved and Appraise is skippable; the design protected only the smaller ≤0.5σ armature against lookup.
cite: params/contest.md §Faction Boosts; sim/personal/contest/dictionaries.py guilds_boost_for; social_contest_v30.md §4 Step 1; sim/.../appraise.py

claim: The strain→Rattled→Face channel is unwired, so losing exchanges carry no cumulative cost and the ratified stamina/standing/merits three-tracker tradeoff is decorative; dump Focus/Spirit is optimal.
cite: social_contest_v30.md §4 Step 6 honesty note + §8 Stage-1d status; params/contest.md §Face and Rattled

claim: Canon itself states splitting dominates concentration by +9–45% win-rate with the sole exceptions of Narrow Pass terrain or the Size=1 edge, so the headline concentrate-vs-split tactical axis is a solved (non-dominant-violating) choice — resolved in ED-358 as 'intended' without ever applying the Omega non-dominance lens.

Domain Echo's cap is per-scene not per-season, so Debate-scene spam farms hegemon Mandate up and rival Mandate down simultaneously — the exact quantities the GD-1 institutional-domination clause reads — with no count limit found; §5.3 only fixes timing.

Incumbents can strip insurgent Presence markers by rote (Crown Enforce, Military vs Ob 2, -1 marker/season), permanently choking the Revolt->Insurgency->Faction emergence pipeline and the 'found your own faction' North-Star promise; only partially self-limited by Enforce's Order -1 cost.
cite: victory_v30.md §3.5 (Enforce Ob 2; RM spread only at Stability>=3; marker cap 5)

claim: Mending self-contradicts on Coherence (§3.2 table -1 vs §2.4 asymmetry 0); under the canonical 0 it strictly dominates the RS/world-stewardship sub-tree — Coherence-free, RS+ and Mandate+ and unopposeable — so 'which op for the world' collapses to 'Mend'.
cite: threadwork_v30.md §3.2 line 624 vs §2.4 lines 476-481; params/threadwork.md §Mending Immunity + §Mending Community Quality Tiers; scale_transitions_v30.md §5.6

claim: A practitioner-leader's single Territorial Weave resolves as the Domain Action, raises Stability +1, and fires positive Domain Echo Mandate +1 (one roll, three benefits), dominating conventional Assert — partially checked by negative Church-territory Echo, Gap risk, and practitioner gating.

The entire М-7..М-11 phenomenology meta-tier — T-31/33/34/35/37/38/39/40 — is declared canonical in throughlines_complete.md but implemented by no ratified subsystem head; grep for its signature vocabulary hits only designs/audit synthesis and workplan docs, never threadwork_v30 or any design head, so a whole throughline limb is registry-resident and mechanically absent.

COLLISION C — the charter's and T-24's own worked example of emergence (RS+8/IP+2/TC+2) — is triggered by ARC-T04 'Southernmost Ritual [UNNAMED — ED-416]', an unnamed and unresolved design item, so the canonical illustration of the emergent engine rests on a design hole.
